string.text:
The BS-1 RGA-86 "Tishina" is a Silenced Soviet 30mm grenade launcher, designed in the 1960s and produced from the 1970s. Made to be used for the AKS-74UB, the grenade launcher offers another firepower option for said weapon, all the while still being somewhat silent compared to the GP-25's VOG-25. It is operated by using a blank 5.45x39mm round, which is deposited in a magazine, and chambered by bolt-action. It was meant to be used by the Spetsnaz, as a method to make ambushes without being seen, even if a Grenade Launcher was used.
 
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] muzzle velocity: %c[ui_gray_1]~105 m / sec
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] fire range: %c[ui_gray_1]~400 m
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] reload time: %c[ui_gray_1]slowest
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] ammunition: %c[ui_gray_1]VOG-T 30mm grenade

The VOG-T is a 30mm grenade meant to be used with the BS-1 RGA-86 "Tishina". Said grenade is smaller in comparison to its older brother, the VOG-25, at 40mm vs 30mm, this makes the VOG-T slightly weaker in power, but also small enough to fit into said Grenade Launcher, as well as being less louder than the VOG-25.
 
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] Blast power: %c[ui_gray_1]small
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] Blast radius: %c[ui_gray_1]3.0 m
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] Fragments' radius: %c[ui_gray_1]10 m
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] Av. number of fragments: %c[ui_gray_1]50 (small shrapnels)
 %c[d_purple] • %c[ui_gray_2] Explosion delay: %c[ui_gray_1]none (explodes on impact)
